3D UNet refiner — results (training in progress)

Does the refiner deblur the splat? Snapshot 2026-06-17: frozen run epoch ~62 (≈converged), joint run epoch ~22 (still climbing). How-it-works: 09_unet_refiner.html; why: 08_breathing_failure_mode.html.

TL;DR — the refiner works.
  • Frozen run (geometry fixed, refiner-only): V_canon pinned at 26.41 bbox; V_refined = 27.91 → the refiner adds +1.50 dB bbox / +0.98 dB motion, and it has plateaued (≈converged). This is a clean, isolated splat-deblur gain.
  • Sharper, not smoother — but modestly. V_canon sharpness 0.668× GT → V_refined 0.702× (+0.034); bbox PSNR 26.49→28.04 (n=60). V_refined IS sharper (so it's not just blurring), but the sharpness gain is small: the +1.5 dB is mostly accuracy / coverage-error correction (filling dim & under-covered voxels, fixing splat artifacts) with only a little high-frequency recovery — exactly what L1 (mean-seeking) predicts. A gradient/SSIM loss should lift the sharpness component (see §6).
  • Deblurring the splat > training the geometry more. The refiner adds +1.50 dB on a FIXED geometry (the var2 ep-59 seed). For comparison, the no-refiner baseline (var2) trained ~25 epochs further kept its raw-splat V_canon stuck in the 26.4–27.1 dB band (≈+0.5 at most). So deblurring the splat is the bigger, more efficient lever — the splat, not the geometry, was the bottleneck (report 08). (Caveat: the two geometries differ, so this is a lever-efficiency comparison, not a head-to-head.)
  • Joint run (geometry + refiner co-adapting) is younger (epoch ~22); V_canon 26.31 / V_refined 27.36 and both still rising — preliminary but trending positive.
What is / isn't conclusive yet. Conclusive: the refiner deblurs — on a fixed geometry it adds ~+1.5 dB bbox / ~+1 dB motion and raises sharpness, and it has converged. Not yet conclusive: the JOINT run's final number (still climbing at epoch ~22/200) and the L1-vs-sharpness-loss ceiling (the frozen plateau at ~27.91 suggests L1 caps the gain — a gradient/SSIM term may lift it further).

1. The three runs

rungeometry (VGGT)refinerrole
frozen (51876098)frozen at var2 ep59 (pinned)trainsisolates the pure splat-deblur gain
joint (51876099)finetunes from VGGT-1B basetrainsgeometry + refiner co-adapt
var2 baseline (51862799)finetunes (resumed ep59→)noneno-refiner control (V_canon only)

2. Trajectories — refiner (solid) vs raw splat (dashed)

In the frozen run the blue dashed line (V_canon) is flat — geometry is frozen, so the raw splat can't change — while the blue solid line (V_refined) jumps ~1.5 dB above it: that gap is the refiner. In the joint run both rise (green); var2 (grey dotted) is the no-refiner control.

Motion PSNR (dynamic/heart voxels) — same pattern.

3. Is it a real deblur? (sharpness)

Sharpness = in-plane gradient energy ÷ GT (1.0 = as sharp as GT). If V_refined only smoothed, sharpness would fall; instead it rises (0.668→0.702) — so it's not blurring. But the rise is modest: most of the +1.5 dB is accuracy/coverage correction, not high-frequency recovery. Under L1 (which regresses to the blurry mean) that's expected — a sharpness-aware loss (§6) is the lever to recover more detail.

4. Qualitative (breathing val, frozen checkpoint)

Same inputs through V_canon (raw splat) vs V_refined. Compare row 2 vs row 3; row 4 is the residual error. Check that V_refined sharpens real structure and doesn't fabricate anatomy.

refiner_seq0_t0.png — rows: V_gt / V_canon (raw splat) / V_refined / signed diff. Compare row 2 vs row 3.

refiner_seq7_t7.png — rows: V_gt / V_canon (raw splat) / V_refined / signed diff. Compare row 2 vs row 3.

5. Conclusions

  • The refiner is the right fix. On a fixed geometry it recovers ~+1.50 dB bbox and raises sharpness toward GT — directly attacking the splat blur that report 08 showed was ~75% of the error.
  • Splat-deblur is the more efficient lever. A refiner on a fixed geometry adds ~+1.5 dB, whereas training that geometry ~25 epochs further (var2) moved its raw splat only ~+0.5 (staying in the 26.4–27.1 band). The splat — not the geometry — was the bottleneck (report 08). (Different geometries, so a lever-efficiency comparison, not head-to-head.)
  • Deep supervision held: in the joint run V_canon keeps improving (the point head stays supervised by L_pre) while V_refined leads — the refiner isn't making the geometry lazy.

6. Next steps

  • Let the joint run converge (epoch ~22/200) and re-snapshot — it should exceed the frozen number once its geometry catches up.
  • Add a sharpness-aware loss term (gradient L1 / 1−SSIM) — the frozen plateau (~27.91) suggests L1 caps the deblur; a sharpness term should push V_refined higher.
  • Then consider a larger refiner / learned decoder, and an out-of-distribution / hallucination audit on held-out subjects.

Reproduce: trajectories parsed from slurm_logs/*refiner*; tools/eval_refiner.py (sharpness + panels on the frozen checkpoint); _html/build_refiner_results_report.py (this page).
